commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=59ca8e586bd03df782343406f87cce23fa939033)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Claude live-session AskUserQuestion handling, prompts reply-capable surfaces for answers, sends a no-surface plain-text fallback, and adds focused spawn tests.

PR surface: Source +378, Tests +211. Total +589 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, though I did not execute the runtime: current main routes Claude `can_use_tool` requests through exec approval only, and the PR defects follow from the changed answer serialization plus documented queue routing. The related open report at https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/81099 also matches the stalled AskUserQuestion behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human author or maintainer follow-up is needed because the branch has blocking runtime defects and the contributor still needs to supply real reply-surface proof.

target.title: [codex] fix Claude AskUserQuestion bridge
target.body:
## Summary

- Handle Claude `AskUserQuestion` control requests in the Claude live stdio runner instead of routing them through the native exec approval path.
- Render Claude question/options payloads as plain reply-surface prompts, then map the next active-run queued message back into Claude's documented `updatedInput.answers` shape.
- Add a deterministic no-surface fallback that denies `AskUserQuestion` and tells Claude to ask directly in plain text.
- Cover both the bridged prompt path and no-surface fallback in the Claude live-session spawn tests.

## Root Cause

Claude sends `AskUserQuestion` through the same `can_use_tool` control request channel as native tool approval. OpenClaw's Claude live-session handler only treated those requests as permission decisions, and CLI reply backends did not expose a queued-message path for user answers. On surfaces without an interactive picker, Claude's structured question could not be answered deterministically.

## Impact

Agents like Sam can now ask interview/clarifying questions through normal OpenClaw reply surfaces. If a surface cannot collect structured input, Claude gets a clear plain-text fallback instruction instead of depending on an unsupported picker.

## Verification

- `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `git diff --cached --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.spawn.test.ts` -> 58 passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Claude `AskUserQuestion` prompts in live stdio CLI runs now have deterministic OpenClaw handling: reply-capable runs can bridge the question through the reply surface, and no-surface runs tell Claude to fall back to plain text instead of depending on an unsupported picker.

Real environment tested: Clean PR worktree at `/Users/tim/JSI/GIT/openclaw-claude-ask-user-question` based on `upstream/main` (`c4a0ca0b7a413207ded7b8d07c86cc30e004ebdd`), running OpenClaw `2026.6.2 (59ca8e5)` from this branch. The live smoke used an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME` and the machine's real Claude CLI (`claude 2.1.168`) with `claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6` available.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`; `git diff --cached --check`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.spawn.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`; `OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-proof-home.SjmIiJ corepack pnpm openclaw agent --local --agent main --model claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6 --session-key agent:main:ask-user-question-proof --message "This is a runtime smoke test for OpenClaw. Call the AskUserQuestion tool now with one question exactly 'Which mode should OpenClaw use?' and two options exactly 'Plan' and 'Build'. If the host denies that tool or says to ask in plain text, do not ask a follow-up; instead reply exactly 'ASK_USER_QUESTION_FALLBACK_OBSERVED'." --timeout 180 --json`.

Evidence after fix: The live OpenClaw command produced a Claude CLI live-session run and the expected fallback sentinel. Relevant redacted output:

```text
OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (59ca8e5)
[agent/cli-backend] cli exec: provider=claude-cli model=claude-sonnet-4-6 promptChars=351 trigger=user useResume=false session=none resumeSession=none reuse=none historyPrompt=none
[agent/cli-backend] claude live session start: provider=claude-cli model=claude-sonnet-4-6 activeSessions=1
[agent/cli-backend] claude live session turn: provider=claude-cli model=claude-sonnet-4-6 durationMs=8463 rawLines=41 outBytes=35 outHash=80b95109022b
"finalAssistantVisibleText": "ASK_USER_QUESTION_FALLBACK_OBSERVED"
"executionTrace": { "winnerProvider": "claude-cli", "winnerModel": "claude-sonnet-4-6", "runner": "cli" }
```

Observed result after fix: In the live no-surface path, Claude attempted the requested `AskUserQuestion` behavior, received the OpenClaw fallback instruction, and completed with `ASK_USER_QUESTION_FALLBACK_OBSERVED` through a real Claude CLI-backed OpenClaw run. In focused runtime coverage, a simulated reply-capable run writes a plain prompt via `onUserInputPrompt`, `queueReplyRunMessage` answers the pending question, and the Claude control response includes `updatedInput.answers["Which thread is this?"] === "All"`.

What was not tested: A live external chat surface round trip where a user answers the bridged prompt through Telegram/Slack/etc. was not run; that interactive reply-surface path is covered by the focused Claude live-session runtime test.
target.author: jsi-tross

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
